label: Annual screening capacity per installed site
note: Conservative annual throughput range for a primary-care tele-retinal workflow using scheduled and opportunistic diabetes visits.

label: Gradable image share in baseline screening workflow
note: Share of completed screening encounters that produce an interpretable exam in a routine primary-care retinal workflow.

label: Fundus + AI throughput multiplier
note: Automation improves gradability triage and clinician time efficiency, but the effect is bounded.

label: Fundus + AI gradability multiplier
note: Automation and standardized acquisition support can modestly increase the share of interpretable exams.

label: Fundus + OCT adjunct throughput multiplier
note: Adjunct OCT improves diagnostic richness but usually slows throughput versus fundus-only screening.

label: Fundus + OCT adjunct gradability multiplier
note: Adjunct OCT can improve the rate of clinically useful exams and add confidence around macular disease.

label: OCT-only advanced throughput multiplier
note: Used only for the advanced exploratory scenario because the evidence base is weaker for OCT-only diabetic retinopathy screening in primary care.

label: OCT-only advanced gradability multiplier
note: Exploratory proxy reflecting OCT interpretability advantages while still treating OCT-only primary-care screening as lower-confidence overall.

label: Existing staff deployment modifier
note: Existing staff can implement screening, but throughput and adoption are limited without protected workflow capacity.

label: Trained MA/RN deployment modifier
note: A trained rooming or care-management workflow is treated as the reference implementation.

label: Dedicated coordinator deployment modifier
note: A dedicated coordinator boosts screening capture, patient re-engagement, and follow-up completion.

label: Local ophthalmology follow-up completion
note: Local referral completion is often constrained by appointment delays and transportation burden.

label: Regional hub follow-up completion
note: Hub scheduling and referral management tend to improve follow-up reliability in rural networks.

label: Tele-ophthalmology network follow-up completion
note: Virtual specialty coordination and shorter wait times typically improve follow-up conversion.

label: Treatment or monitored management after confirmed follow-up
note: Share of patients completing retinal follow-up who move into timely treatment or monitored management rather than remaining untreated.

label: Sustained management after treatment start
note: Share of treatment starts or monitored-management starts that remain active long enough to plausibly change three-year harm trajectories.

label: Referable diabetic retinopathy prevalence
note: Default share of screened patients expected to have referable retinopathy or closely related retinal findings.

label: Care re-engagement from screening encounter
note: Share of screened patients who re-enter broader diabetes management because retinal screening creates a new touchpoint.

label: Additional care re-engagement from confirmed eye follow-up
note: Extra share of patients who re-enter broader diabetes care because a positive screen converts into confirmed specialty follow-up and renewed clinical attention.

label: Re-engaged patients achieving improved control
note: Synthetic bridge assumption translating care re-engagement into durable diabetes management improvement.

label: Annual diabetes-rate bridge share
note: Synthetic bridge translating improved control into an indicative reduction in modeled adult diabetes rate versus baseline trajectory.

label: Fundus screening device capex per site
note: Represents an indicative Topcon-compatible fundus screening deployment cost range before training and connectivity.

label: AI license and reading workflow cost per site
note: Used for the fundus + FDA-cleared AI scenario as an annual software and support proxy.

label: OCT adjunct capex per site
note: Indicative incremental cost for adding OCT capability to a primary-care retinal workflow.

label: Training and onboarding cost per site
note: Training covers staff time, workflow setup, and implementation coaching for the first year.

label: Connectivity and cybersecurity cost per site
note: Proxy for broadband, cybersecurity, and data-transfer support in rural implementation environments.

label: Annual device maintenance rate
note: Annual maintenance and service share applied to equipment capex.

label: Average reimbursement or funded value per screen
note: Blended reimbursement or funded screening value used for indicative ROI modeling.

label: Avoided downstream cost per earlier case identified
note: Indicative avoided cost per earlier retinal case detected and routed into treatment sooner.

label: Annual savings per re-engaged diabetes patient
note: Proxy for avoided utilization and improved care management in patients re-engaged through the screening program.

label: Maximum diabetic population coverage share
note: Upper bound on how much of the diabetic population can realistically be reached by deployed primary-care sites in the modeled horizon.

label: Baseline follow-up completion share
note: Share of already screened referable cases expected to complete eye-care follow-up without the new deployment.

label: Three-year progression into major vision-threatening consequences
note: Three-year share of delayed referable retinal cases expected to progress into major preventable vision-threatening harm without timely management.

label: Severe consequence avoidance share from timely management
note: Conservative share of major preventable vision-threatening harm avoided when delayed cases are moved into timely retinal management.

label: Blindness or profound vision loss share of severe consequences
note: Share of major preventable retinal consequences assumed to culminate in blindness or profound permanent vision loss absent earlier detection and treatment.

label: Late-stage surgery share of severe consequences
note: Proxy share of major preventable harm expected to require vitrectomy-scale surgery or other invasive rescue intervention.

label: QALY gain per severe vision-loss case avoided over three years
note: Indicative three-year quality-of-life gain assigned to a major vision-loss case avoided before full blindness occurs.

label: QALY gain per blindness case avoided over three years
note: Indicative three-year quality-of-life gain for preventing blindness or profound permanent vision loss.

label: DALY burden avoided per severe vision-loss case over three years
note: Indicative disability burden avoided when a major but non-blinding vision-loss event is prevented.

label: DALY burden avoided per blindness case over three years
note: Indicative three-year disability burden avoided when blindness or profound permanent vision loss is prevented.

label: Working-age share used for productivity value
note: Share of prevented severe vision-loss cases assumed to carry direct labor-force and productivity exposure in the modeled horizon.

label: Annual productivity value protected per major vision-loss case
note: Conservative annual productivity value protected per major vision-loss case avoided, framed as a subset of the broader economic burden of vision loss.
